title: Evangelion Double Feature
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Double Feature             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:43 am If I show someone Death/Rebirth and then EoE, will the former provide them enough context to understand the latter? (I suppose I should be able to answer this myself, but the only impression Death left on me was of how surprisingly good the dub was.)

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:54 am They could probably have enought of a general idea to grasp the background behind the characters and their interactions as well as some vague idea of how they got to that point, but frankly i personally wouldn't use Death/Rebirth as anything other than re-cap or compilation to be appreciated by people who have already seen the series proper. So if i had to choose, i'd say "no".

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Mar 06, 2015 1:24 am I'd say no, too. They might have a grasp of a story, but all the emotional impact pretty much evaporates.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Fri Mar 06, 2015 2:04 am I have to agree with what Dream and robersora said. I actually like Death & Rebirth. I consider it to be a bit like a required pop quiz for anyone who has already seen the series. You need to sit through it before attempting to tackle EoE. If your memory of episodes 1 thru 24 is good enough to follow D&R's disjointed approach of "we're gonna show you multiple events from the series out of order, jump around chronologically, and apply soundbites from early episodes as emotionally effective voiceovers to scenes from completely different episodes later on, pausing only for the occasional musical interlude," then you are truly ready to proceed (with caution).

[]: Re: Evangelion Double Feature             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 3:21 am Tumbling Down wrote: If I show someone Death/Rebirth and then EoE, will the former provide them enough context to understand the latter? (I suppose I should be able to answer this myself, but the only impression Death left on me was of how surprisingly good the dub was.)

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Fri Mar 06, 2015 7:14 am I have to agree with everyone, it would be best to show your friend the actual series first. It would be a shame not to allow them to enjoy the wonderful tonal shift the series goes through and the descent arc.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 06, 2015 7:53 am Just this pass week I had a bunch of friends visit me, some traveling from out of state. I was introducing when of them local friends to Eva, and we were finally approaching EoE. I decided to make his exposure to EoE a shared experience, so I invited everyone else. One of my friends had already scene NGE, EoTV, Death, and EoE. Another had only seen NGE, EoTV, and EoE. Yet another had seen only about half of NGE. And the person I was introducing Eva to was caught up on NGE and EoTV, and was just breaking into Death and EoE. It was all of our first times watching the 2003 Renewal edition of the films from Japan. Death helped the friend who saw only half of NGE, and he really appreciated the fact that he saw it before moving on to EoE. The rest of them appreciated Death well enough simply because it certainly does allow you to keep certain important memories of NGE fresh without needing to rematch the entire series again, and watch EoE. But I can’t imagine Death + EoE doing well for an audience who either wasn’t familiar with Eva or wasn’t aware of the fact that this concludes an entire TV series. I have a feeling that reactions would be split down the middle if they were introduced in that way. (But then again, reactions to NGE are split down the middle by the end of it all anyway, so why not just cut the crap and skip to the devision? )

[]: Re: Evangelion Double Feature             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 06, 2015 8:07 am Tumbling Down wrote: If I show someone Death and then EoE, will the former provide them enough context to understand the latter?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 06, 2015 8:48 am Oddly enough, I don’t think anyone starting with Death would understand that there were things about Eva that needed to be understood to begin with. You need to ask these questions before you can answer them.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Fri Mar 06, 2015 9:22 am Short answer:no, long answer:hell no. Series, then you have a choice, death+rebirth then EoE or straight to EoE

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 9:25 am Plus the choice of whether to visit both the TV and DC versions of 21-24 on the way, of course!

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Mar 07, 2015 11:38 pm I barely understood Death/Rebirth the first time I saw it, which happened to be immediately after binge-watching the complete run of episodes 1-24. So, no. Don't start someone there.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Mar 08, 2015 12:08 am user-02 wrote: I barely understood Death/Rebirth the first tine I saw it, which happened to be immediately after binge-watching the complete run of episodes 1-24. So, no. Don't start someone there.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sun Mar 08, 2015 9:09 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Death is honestly one of my favorite things to come from the original series. I understand it completely. But then, I’ve slept with Death. So of course I understand it that well.
summary:
[]: Evangelion Double Feature             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Mar 06, 2015 12:43 am If I show someone Death/Rebirth and then EoE, will the former provide them enough context to understand the latter? (I suppose I should be able to answer this myself, but the only impression Death left on me was of how surprisingly good the dub was.)

[]:             Quote Post by Dream
